mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth above 2% annually signals a healthy expansion that supports corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth below 2% still produces positive returns in 60% of quarters, or rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 5% indicates labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% from its current level
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts and retail sales signal a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, which amplifies the negative impact on economic growth and equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both proxies decline, forward returns are positive in at least 50% of instances, indicating no additional predictive power from the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and annual CPI inflation is below 3%, the monetary environment is accommodative. In this regime, the discount rate effect dominates and equity markets tend to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 3%
regime_hint: near-zero interest rate environment with benign inflation
failure_condition: In at least 30% of months when trigger is active, forward returns are negative, or the average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates an imminent contraction in manufacturing activity, which typically precedes broader economic weakness and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines, forward returns are positive in more than half of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflect deteriorating conditions in both the labor market and housing sector, which compound each other's negative effects on economic confidence and consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and HOUST decline month-over-month
failure_condition: When both proxies decline, forward returns over 60 days are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the compound effect is not more negative than individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points within a month signals unexpected monetary tightening, which immediately raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, causing negative 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 bp in one month
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward 5-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPI greater than 0.3% indicates rising inflation pressure, which forces the Fed to consider tightening monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than half of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.
